Obstetric History Obstetric History not reviewed (last reviewed 10/14/2025) Past Medical History Past Medical History not reviewed (last reviewed 10/14/2025) HPI This 80-year-old female is being seen at home for follow-up on chronic health conditions. She denies any chest pain, shortness of breath or palpitations. Has request for medication refills. She is requesting to switch to Lasix as she states that hctz is not assisting with the bilateral lower extremity edema. The patient has lymphedema, sits up most of the time with her lower ext dependent despite education regarding the need for elevation of her lower extremities. Functionally, she states it is difficult to elevate her legs, she has been encouraged to lay down in the bed periodically during the day to assist with the edema. Again reiterated fluid restriction. Discussed need for labs to monitor her renal function due to the requested change in her diuretic. The patient is requesting podiatry evaluation for calluses on her feet which she is unable to manage on her own, also is requesting physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status. The patient previously had a live-in caregiver however due to difficulties with the person, she is now living alone, she has a friend who does come almost daily to assist her. The patient is still in the process of trying to find provider to gct a power chair, she requires it for assistance with ADLs in her home. She currently has 1 which is too large and makes it difficult for her to be able to remain independent and maneuver it in her home. Now that she has no live in caregiver, she is needing to be more independent and have abilities to take care of her ADLs on her own. Functionally, she is unable to use a standard wheelchair as she is unableto propel it due to arthritis in her shoulders and also due to her excessive weight. The wheelchair is too wide for her home. The patient has the mental and physical capabilities to operate a power chair, she has 1 now and is able to use it safely in her home. Her home does have adequate access between the rooms and maneuvering space for her to use a chair. Using the power chair improves her ability to participate in her ADLs as she is home alone and she has proven the ability to use one to remain independent as she lives alone. The patient has limited ambulation abilities due to morbid obesity, BLE lymphedema and advanced arthritis in her knees and PN. A cane and walker do not meet her needs as it does not provide enough support and the patient is unable to ambulate more than a few steps to transfer making her a fall risk. She is able to stand from a seated position without assistance and get on her current power chair, she gets short of breath with ambulation and only is able to ambulate a few steps due to exertion as well as lower extremity pain. athena 3/26/2026 3:06:31 pm EDT c3bd0f9e-5759-4372-8bbb-301f5f2ebddb Page: 06 / 13 P3 Health Partners NV 2370 Corporate Circle, HENDERSON NV 89074-7760 ROOT, Elizabeth (id #367, dob: 04/20/1945) ROS Additionally reports: General: Denies fever, chills, fatigue, night sweats, weight loss, weight gain. Eyes: Denies eye pain, floaters or visual disturbances. ENT: Denies ear pain, runny nose, or sore throat. Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath, wheezing or cough. Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain, palpitations +lower extremity edema. Gastrointestinal: Denies abdominal pain, N/V/D or constipation. Genitourinary: Denies painful urination. Denies urine leakage or hematuria. + urgency/freq on diuretic Musculoskeletal: Denies muscle or joint pain. Neurological: Denies headache, dizziness or difficulty with coordinating movements. Skin: Denies rash or lesions Psychiatric: Denies anxiety, depression or sleep disturbances. Endocrine: Denies excessive thirst or temperature intolerance. Hematologic/Lymphatic: Denies swollen glands, easy bruising or bleeding. Physical Exam GENERAL: Well developed, well nourished morbidly obese female in no acute distress. Alert, oriented x4. HEAD: NCAT, PERRLA. LUNGS: CTAB, resp eupneic, sl decreased BS bilat. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi. HEART: RRR with no murmurs, gallops, heave or thrill appreciated. ABDOMEN: Soft, obese, NT, ND, BS active, no masses palpable-difficult to assess as pt sitting in chair. EXT: -C/C, 2+ BLE LE, pulses difficult to palpate SKIN: Pk, warm, dry and intact. No rashes or lesions noted. NEURO: No acute focal deficits noted. PSYCH: Affect and mood appropriate, pleasant. Assessment / Plan This 80-year-old female is being seen at home for f/u on chronic health conditions. Time spent >60 min for face to face home visit, chart review, med reconciliation, pt education and documentation. 1. Primary gonarthrosis, bilateral - Stable, continue gabapentin. Will continue supportive care. M17.0: Bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knee gabapentin 300 mg capsule To be submitted on or around 03/19/2026 TAKE 1 CAPSULE BY MOUTH THREE TIMES DAILY Qty: (180) capsule Pharmacy: WALGREENS DRUG STORE #10215 2. Chronic venous stasis dermatitis Discussed elevation of lower extremities and avoidance of prolonged dependent position of extremities. Discussed skin care to avoid cracks and development of open wounds. Monitor. 187.2: Venous insufficiency (chronic) (peripheral) 3. Impaired mobility Patient able to transfer but has very limited ability to ambulate, she is only able to take a few steps. Is requesting a power chair for use in her home to perform self ADLs, is unable to stand for more than a few seconds. She currently has a power chair that she has paid for however it is too big for her home, is requesting a smaller chair so that she has more access in her home to maintain her independence.
